circular.title: Reserve Bank of India (Commercial Banks – Cash Reserve Ratio and Statutory Liquidity Ratio) Directions, 2025
circular.summary: These Directions mandate the maintenance of Cash Reserve Ratio (CRR) and Statutory Liquidity Ratio (SLR) by all commercial banks in India. Banks are required to maintain a specified percentage of their Net Demand and Time Liabilities (NDTL) as cash balances with the RBI (CRR) and as liquid assets like gold or government securities (SLR). Non-compliance results in significant penal interest charges and potential regulatory action against bank management.

clauses.text: These Directions shall be called the Reserve Bank of India (Commercial Banks – Cash Reserve Ratio and Statutory Liquidity Ratio) Directions, 2025.
clauses.summary: Short title and commencement of the Directions

clauses.text: These Directions apply to all Commercial Banks, including SBI and corresponding new banks, but excluding SFBs, Local Area Banks, and Payments Banks.
Every bank shall maintain in India a cash reserve as a percentage of its NDTL as specified by RBI under Section 42(1) of the RBI Act, 1934, and Section 18(1) of the BR Act, 1949.
Every bank shall maintain assets in India valued at no less than the percentage specified by RBI (not exceeding 40%) of its demand and time liabilities.

obligations.text: Maintain average daily CRR balance as prescribed (3% - 3.75% of NDTL depending on the period)
obligations.plain_english: Banks must maintain a minimum cash balance with the RBI

obligations.text: Maintain minimum daily CRR balance of at least 90% of the requirement
obligations.plain_english: Banks must maintain a minimum daily cash balance

obligations.text: Maintain SLR assets (Cash, Gold, or Approved Securities) at no less than 18% of NDTL
obligations.plain_english: Banks must maintain a minimum level of liquid assets

prohibitions.text: Failure to maintain CRR or SLR
prohibitions.plain_english: Banks are not allowed to fail to maintain the required cash and liquid asset levels

penalties.amount: Penal interest under Section 24 of the BR Act, 1949

definitions.definition: Aggregation of demand and time deposits
definitions.plain_english: Total deposits held by a bank

definitions.definition: Dated G-Secs, T-Bills, State Development Loans (SDLs), and other notified instruments
definitions.plain_english: Securities that can be held as part of the SLR
